meaning: n.横贯,横断,横木,障碍,否认,反驳,(建筑)通廊vt.横过,穿过,经过,在...来回移动,反对,详细研究vi.横越,横断,旋转,来回移动adj.横断的,横的
examples: They don't like dense forests either, so they traverse these high, open shoulders between the river and the woods, where they can see and run.
In their cross-country journey, the animals must traverse many smaller bodies of water, such as rivers and small lakes.
Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run.
The 85-year-old National Cherry Festival in Traverse City, for example, attracts 500, 000 visitors every July.
True potential is latent in the rugged terrain that few are willing to traverse.
